title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Probe Connector Damage or Probe Not Recognized
description: Guide for troubleshooting probe recognition issues and possible connector damage on a Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the Edge II and unplug AC power if connected. Wait 30–60 seconds, then restart the system. Reason: Resets internal communication between the system and probe.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Connector and Cable
steps.instructions: Check for bent pins, debris, corrosion, or visible damage on the probe connector. Inspect the probe cable for cuts, fraying, or kinks. Reason: Physical damage can prevent proper connection and recognition.

steps.title: Clean the Connector Contacts
steps.instructions: Use a lint-free cloth or approved isopropyl alcohol wipe on the probe connector and system port. Ensure the system is powered off during cleaning. Reason: Dirt or residue can block proper electrical contact.

steps.title: Reconnect the Probe Firmly
steps.instructions: Insert the probe fully into the system port until it clicks or seats properly. Avoid twisting or forcing the connector. Reason: Loose connections are a common cause of 'not detected' errors.

steps.title: Test with Another Compatible Probe
steps.instructions: If available, connect a different, known-working probe to the same port. If the new probe is recognized, the original probe is likely faulty. Reason: Isolates whether the issue is the probe or the system connector.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Probe on Another Edge II Unit
steps.instructions: If another Edge II is available, connect the probe there. If it fails on multiple systems, the probe itself is likely defective.

CCR.Complaint: Probe not detected on the Edge II.
CCR.Cause: Probe connector showed bent pins and intermittent contact; system did not recognize probe.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned connector, tested with another probe, confirmed original probe faulty; labeled probe Out of Service and sent for repair.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Image Artifacts or Degraded Image Quality
description: Guide to troubleshoot image artifacts or degraded quality on Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound systems, focusing on external causes first.

steps.title: Verify Probe Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the probe is fully seated in the connector. Loose or partially connected probes can cause artifacts. Inspect the connector for bent pins or debris.

steps.title: Swap or Inspect the Probe
steps.instructions: Test with a known-good probe to see if the artifact persists. If the image improves, the original probe may be damaged.

steps.title: Check the Cable and Strain Relief
steps.instructions: Examine the probe cable for kinks, cuts, or frayed areas. Damaged cables can cause intermittent signal loss.

steps.title: Review System Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm presets and imaging modes are correct for the intended study. Reset the image settings to default to rule out incorrect gain, depth, or frequency settings.

steps.title: Check the Display and Monitor Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect video output cables and monitor connections for loose connections or damage. Confirm the display itself is functioning correctly by swapping monitors if available.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn the system off, wait 30–60 seconds, then restart. This can resolve temporary software glitches affecting image processing.

steps.title: Run Built-in Self-Test / Diagnostic Tools
steps.instructions: Utilize the Edge II’s internal diagnostics to check for probe or system errors. Document any error codes for escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound images appear grainy and show streaking artifacts during exams.
CCR.Cause: Probe was properly seated but the same artifact persisted on multiple probes; system presets verified; cables and display connections intact.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Battery Charging Issues
description: Troubleshooting guide for battery charging issues on the Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system, focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the system is plugged into a known working outlet. Test the outlet with another device or a voltmeter. Reason: A faulty outlet or power strip is a common cause of battery not charging.

steps.title: Check Power Cord and Adapter
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord for cuts, frays, or bent connectors. Confirm the AC adapter matches the specifications for the Edge II. Swap with a known-good cord/adapter if available. Reason: Damaged cords or incorrect adapters prevent proper charging.

steps.title: Confirm Battery Seating
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the battery in the device. Ensure the battery clicks fully into place and terminals align correctly. Reason: Poor contact can prevent charging or cause intermittent detection.

steps.title: Inspect Battery for Damage
steps.instructions: Look for swelling, leaks, corrosion, or unusual heat. Reason: Physical damage indicates the battery should be removed from service.

steps.title: Observe System Indicators
steps.instructions: Check the on-screen battery icon or LEDs. Note any error codes or messages displayed when connected to AC. Reason: System diagnostics may identify whether the issue is the battery or device circuitry.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Battery
steps.instructions: If available, insert a different, fully charged Edge II battery. Observe if it charges normally. Reason: Confirms whether the issue is with the battery or the system.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Shut down the ultrasound, remove AC power and battery, wait 1–2 minutes, then reconnect and power on. Reason: Resets charging circuitry and clears minor firmware glitches.

CCR.Complaint: Battery not charging, system only works on AC power.
CCR.Cause: Observed that the AC outlet and adapter are functioning, battery seated correctly, but the system does not charge. Battery indicator remains static.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Battery inspected: Yes, no visible damage
Error messages observed: None or specific battery alert
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - Buttons or Keypad Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot unresponsive or malfunctioning buttons and keypad on Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system.

steps.title: Confirm System Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the ultrasound system is powered on and the battery has sufficient charge or the unit is connected to AC power. Why: A low battery or unstable power can cause input lag or unresponsive keys.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Keypad
steps.instructions: Check for visible debris, liquid contamination, or damage around keys. Why: Dirt, dust, or spills can prevent proper key contact.

steps.title: Clean the Keypad
steps.instructions: Use a lint-free cloth lightly dampened with isopropyl alcohol to clean around and on the keys. Avoid excessive liquid. Why: Cleaning removes dirt and oils that may block electrical contact.

steps.title: Check for External Key Lock or Function Mode
steps.instructions: Verify that any “lock” or “function mode” settings on the system are not engaged (refer to system touchscreen menus). Why: Some ultrasound systems allow locking buttons to prevent accidental presses.

steps.title: Test All Keys Methodically
steps.instructions: Press each button or key individually while observing system response. Note any keys that are non-responsive or inconsistent. Why: Pinpoints which buttons are failing and helps rule out software vs. hardware issues.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled system reboot. Why: Sometimes software glitches can temporarily disable keypad input.

steps.title: Check for Firmware Updates
steps.instructions: Ensure the device firmware is current per manufacturer guidance. Why: Firmware updates can resolve known input or keypad issues.

CCR.Complaint: Several keys on the Edge II keypad do not respond, and imaging cannot be fully controlled.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed no liquid damage. Keypad cleaning and system restart did not restore functionality. Likely internal keypad failure.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for vendor repair.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II - System Boot or Software Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting boot failures, startup errors, and software crashes on the Fujifilm Sonosite Edge II ultrasound system.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the system is plugged into a known working outlet. Check power cords and connections for damage or looseness. Confirm that the AC power indicator is on. Why it matters: Many “boot failures” are actually due to unstable or missing power.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition
steps.instructions: If running on battery, connect AC power and observe LED indicators. Swap with a known good battery if available. Why it matters: Weak or depleted batteries can prevent proper boot.

steps.title: Inspect External Cables and Peripherals
steps.instructions: Disconnect all probes, USB devices, and network cables. Attempt boot with minimal configuration. Why it matters: Faulty peripherals can halt software initialization.

steps.title: Soft Reset / Safe Boot (if available)
steps.instructions: Follow manufacturer’s procedure to perform a soft reset or safe boot mode. Observe any error messages displayed. Why it matters: Safe boot may allow the system to bypass minor software issues.

steps.title: Check for Visible Damage or Overheating
steps.instructions: Inspect for unusual heat, burning smell, or liquid damage. Ensure vents are clear and fan is functional. Why it matters: Overheating or physical damage can prevent proper startup.

steps.title: Observe Error Codes
steps.instructions: Document any messages or codes that appear on startup. Refer to manufacturer support resources for error code meanings.

CCR.Complaint: Edge II will not boot and shows ‘Software Error 101’ on startup.
CCR.Cause: Power confirmed stable, battery verified, no external peripherals attached, soft reset failed; system still displays error.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to the repair bench for evaluation.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite X-Porte Ultrasound System - Probe Not Recognized or Connection Errors
description: Troubleshooting probe detection issues, intermittent connections, or transducer errors caused by connection problems, port faults, or probe failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the probe from patient use and transition imaging to another ultrasound system if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Connect the probe and power on the system. Confirm the probe is not recognized or an error message is displayed.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Connector and Cable
steps.instructions: Check for bent or missing pins, dirt, gel, or debris, cracked housing or worn cable strain relief. Clean using approved methods if needed.

steps.title: Reseat the Probe Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect and firmly reconnect the probe to the X-Porte port. Ensure it is fully inserted and secured.

steps.title: Test with a Known Working Probe
steps.instructions: Connect a different probe that is known to be functional. Second probe works → original probe is faulty. Second probe fails → system or port issue likely.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Probe on Another Compatible System
steps.instructions: If available, test the probe on another Sonosite system. Works elsewhere → X-Porte issue. Fails again → probe failure confirmed.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Port on the X-Porte
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the port for bent or recessed pins, debris or contamination, and physical damage. Do not insert objects into the port.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Power down the X-Porte completely, wait 30 seconds, then restart.

steps.title: Check System Software Behavior
steps.instructions: Observe for additional system errors or abnormal behavior. Ensure the system is not frozen or partially responsive.

CCR.Complaint: Probe not recognized on Sonosite X-Porte ultrasound system.
CCR.Cause: Faulty probe with damaged connector pins.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced probe and confirmed proper recognition and imaging.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite X-Porte Ultrasound System - Image Artifact or Quality Degradation
description: Troubleshooting image artifacts, poor resolution, shadowing, or inconsistent image quality caused by probes, settings, or external factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If image quality is impacting diagnosis, remove the system from patient use and move the patient to a known working ultrasound system. Diagnostic accuracy is critical, and poor imaging can lead to clinical risk.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the system and reproduce the issue. Observe the image for lines, dropouts, or streaks, grainy or low-resolution image, and uneven brightness or shadowing. Expected: Artifact or degradation is visible and repeatable. Why it matters: Confirms a real system issue versus user perception or setup.

steps.title: Check Probe Condition and Cleanliness
steps.instructions: Inspect the probe for cracks in the lens, delamination or bubbling, and residue, gel buildup, or debris. Clean the probe using approved methods. Expected: Probe surface is clean and intact. Why it matters: Probe damage or contamination is the most common cause of image artifacts.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Probe
steps.instructions: Swap the probe with another compatible, known working probe. Expected: If image improves → original probe is faulty. If issue persists → problem is system-related. Why it matters: Quickly isolates probe vs system fault.

steps.title: Inspect Probe Connection and Port
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the probe firmly. Inspect connector for bent or damaged pins and debris or fluid intrusion. Try a different probe port if available. Expected: Secure connection with no visible damage. Why it matters: Poor connections can cause signal loss and artifacts.

steps.title: Check Imaging Settings
steps.instructions: Verify settings such as gain and depth, frequency selection, and preset (correct exam type selected). Reset to a default preset if needed. Expected: Image improves with proper settings. Why it matters: Incorrect configuration can mimic hardware issues.

steps.title: Evaluate Environmental and External Factors
steps.instructions: Check for excessive gel or air gaps, poor probe contact with patient, and electrical interference from nearby equipment. Expected: Stable image when conditions are optimized. Why it matters: External factors can introduce artifacts without device failure.

steps.title: Reboot the System
steps.instructions: Perform a full system restart. Expected: Temporary software-related artifacts may clear. Why it matters: Clears minor software or processing faults.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or System Warnings
steps.instructions: Ensure ventilation is not blocked and system is not overheating. Listen for abnormal fan behavior. Expected: System operates within normal temperature range. Why it matters: Thermal issues can degrade imaging performance.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound image appears grainy with intermittent lines during scanning.
CCR.Cause: Defective probe identified after testing with a known good probe resolved the issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed faulty probe from service and replaced with functional probe; system returned to service.

title: Fujifilm Sonosite X-Porte Ultrasound System - System Fails to Boot or Software Errors
description: Troubleshooting system boot failures, startup freezes, or software errors caused by power, peripherals, or internal issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the system from active patient use. If in use, transfer imaging needs to another ultrasound system before proceeding.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on the system and observe for no power, boot screen freeze, error messages, or repeated restarts.

steps.title: Check Power Source and External Connections
steps.instructions: Verify outlet functionality, ensure power cable is securely connected, and check for power indicator lights.

steps.title: Inspect for Battery-Related Issues
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on using battery only, then reconnect AC power and observe charging indicators.

steps.title: Disconnect All External Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove probes and all peripherals, then power cycle and attempt startup again.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Power off, unplug from AC, wait 60–90 seconds, reconnect power, and restart the system.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure vents are clear, listen for fan operation, and check for excessive heat.

steps.title: Observe Error Messages or Codes
steps.instructions: Document exact wording of any errors and note when they occur during startup.

steps.title: Attempt Alternate Startup (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Retry startup multiple times and observe consistency of failure point.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound system will not boot and displays software error during startup.
CCR.Cause: System failed to complete boot process after removal of external accessories, indicating likely internal software or hardware fault.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for vendor repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Any error messages or codes displayed

title: Fujifilm Sonosite X-Porte Ultrasound System - DICOM or Network Export Failure
description: Troubleshooting failed image export, PACS communication issues, and network-related DICOM failures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot during active patient use. Move to a backup system if imaging/export is urgently needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt export to PACS and note errors such as timeouts, rejection, or no response.

steps.title: Check Network Connection (Physical Layer)
steps.instructions: Inspect Ethernet cable, verify link lights, and test with a known working cable and port.

steps.title: Verify Network Connectivity on the System
steps.instructions: Confirm valid IP, subnet, and gateway. Ensure IP is not 0.0.0.0 or 169.254.x.x and reboot if needed.

steps.title: Test Network Availability
steps.instructions: Use ping or connectivity tools or confirm port activity with IT.

steps.title: Verify DICOM Configuration
steps.instructions: Ensure AE Title, IP, and port match PACS configuration exactly.

steps.title: Check for Recent Changes
steps.instructions: Identify recent network, PACS, or location changes that may affect connectivity.

steps.title: Attempt Alternate Export Method
steps.instructions: Test export to USB or alternate DICOM destination to isolate issue.

steps.title: Reboot the System
steps.instructions: Perform full shutdown and restart to clear temporary network or software faults.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound unable to send images to PACS; export attempts failing.
CCR.Cause: Incorrect DICOM destination IP configuration after network port change.
CCR.Resolution: Updated DICOM settings to correct PACS IP and verified successful image transmission.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: Fujifilm Sonosite X-Porte Ultrasound System - Touchscreen or Control Panel Unresponsive
description: Addresses unresponsive touchscreen or controls due to power, lock settings, external devices, or temporary system/software issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the system from active patient use before troubleshooting. If in use, transfer imaging to another ultrasound system. Why it matters: Prevents interruption of patient care and avoids delays during procedures.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to use both the touchscreen and any physical controls (trackball, buttons). Observe if the entire screen is unresponsive, only certain areas do not respond, or physical controls still work. Expected: Confirm whether the issue is total or partial. Why it matters: Helps differentiate between touchscreen failure and broader system issues.

steps.title: Check for Screen Lock or Software Freeze
steps.instructions: Look for any screen lock icons or inactive UI indicators. Attempt a soft reset by pressing the power button briefly (sleep/wake). Expected: Screen resumes normal responsiveness. Why it matters: Systems can appear frozen due to software lock states.

steps.title: Perform a Full System Reboot
steps.instructions: Power down the system completely. Wait 30–60 seconds, then restart. Expected: Touchscreen and controls respond normally after reboot. Why it matters: Clears temporary software or UI processing errors.

steps.title: Inspect for External Interference or Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect any external USB devices (keyboard, mouse, storage devices). Check for gel, fluid, or debris on the screen or control panel. Clean if needed per manufacturer guidelines. Expected: No obstruction or interference affecting input. Why it matters: External devices or contamination can interfere with touch input.

steps.title: Check Power Stability
steps.instructions: Ensure the system is connected to a reliable power source or has adequate battery charge. Observe for any system lag, flickering, or instability. Expected: Stable power with normal system performance. Why it matters: Low voltage or unstable power can affect system responsiveness.

steps.title: Test Control Panel Functionality
steps.instructions: Use alternate input methods (trackball, keyboard if available). Determine if only the touchscreen is affected. Expected: Other controls function normally if issue is isolated. Why it matters: Helps isolate touchscreen hardware vs full system failure.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Check the touchscreen and control panel for cracks, impact damage, or signs of wear. Expected: No visible damage. Why it matters: Physical damage can cause loss of responsiveness.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen and control panel not responding during imaging use.
CCR.Cause: System software freeze causing loss of touchscreen input.
CCR.Resolution: Performed full system reboot; functionality restored and verified normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Any lag, freezing, or error messages
Final device status (working or removed from service)
